name: Check Point WAF Policy — Administrative Surface Restriction
objective: Restrict access to administrative paths and sensitive support endpoints by combining trusted source controls with explicit URI matching.
risk_mitigated: Exposure of admin interfaces to broad internet traffic, scanning, and opportunistic credential attacks.
potential_side_effects: Remote administrators and third-party support teams may be blocked if their access ranges or jump hosts are not modeled correctly.
tuning_notes: Keep allowlists narrowly scoped and separate VPN, office, and privileged access broker ranges. Pair the policy with authentication telemetry and admin login rate limiting.
deployment_notes: Roll out first on read-only or lower-risk admin surfaces, validate on real operator workflows, and document the emergency bypass process before production enforcement.
monitoring_notes: Monitor blocks against /admin, /manage, /backoffice, and /support URLs. Investigate spikes in denied requests from unfamiliar ASNs or cloud providers.
